mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X7 xDrive30d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned X7 xDrive30d (B57) engine typically costs £4,000–£6,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,500 depending on location and workshop rates. A blown engine in a premium BMW can cost £9,000–£13,000. Main dealer new engines start from £9,000–£13,000+ supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X7 xDrive30d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B57 is widely regarded as one of BMW's most reliable diesel engines, 'celebrated for its robust performance, impressive efficiency, and excellent reliability'. Unlike the N47 and early N57 engines, timing chain issues are 'exceedingly rare'. However, EGR cooler coolant leaks (subject to global BMW recalls), DPF clogging from short journeys, carbon buildup, and oil leaks are well-documented issues.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X7 xDrive30d?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) EGR cooler coolant leaks — the most prevalent problem, subject to global BMW recalls affecting diesel vehicles 2010–2022, (2) Timing chain stretch — 'exceedingly rare' on B57 but documented in high-mileage or poorly serviced examples, (3) DPF clogging — particularly on vehicles used for short urban journeys, and (4) Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and oil filter housing.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X7 xDrive30d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £25,000–£45,000 X7, a £4,500–£6,500 recon is 10–25% of the car's value — worth it given the X7's strong retained values. The B57's robust reliability means that with proper maintenance, these engines can achieve very high mileages.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X7 xDrive30d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The X7 xDrive30d uses the B57D30O0 (also known as B57D30A) — a 3.0-litre single-turbo inline-six diesel producing 265 hp and 620 Nm of torque. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Does the B57 have timing chain issues?

acceptedAnswer.text: Unlike the infamous N47 diesel, the B57's timing chain is 'notably robust and reliable' and issues such as timing chain stretch and guide failures are 'exceedingly rare'. BMW claims the timing chain is a 'lifetime part' and does not service it, but regular oil changes and early attention to warning signs like rattling noises can help prevent costly engine damage. In high-mileage or poorly serviced engines, timing chain stretch can still occur.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW X7 xDrive30d Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an X7 xDrive30d engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The X7's size and complex emissions systems add labour time.

problems.title: EGR Cooler Coolant Leak (Global Recall)
problems.symptoms: White exhaust smoke, rough idling, coolant loss with no visible external leak, burning smell from engine bay, engine warning light, reduction in engine power
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — a manufacturing defect affects multiple BMW diesel models 2010–2022
problems.repairCost: £0 (free under recall) / £600–£1,200 (out of warranty — EGR cooler replacement)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: verify recall completion status immediately; coolant loss can cause serious engine damage and, in extreme cases, fire risk

problems.recommendation: The EGR cooler is one of the most prevalent problems with the B57 engine. A manufacturing defect can cause the EGR cooler to develop an internal coolant leak. The leaking coolant and recirculated exhaust gas may combine, potentially reducing engine power and creating a fire risk. BMW has issued multiple recalls affecting diesel vehicles globally over EGR cooler faults. Verify recall completion before buying any X7 xDrive30d — this is a free fix at the dealership.

failureLink.label: Read the full EGR cooler failure guide

problems.title: Timing Chain Stretch (High-Mileage or Poorly Serviced Engines)
problems.symptoms: Rattling or slapping noise from the engine on cold start, metallic ticking, reduced power, rough idling, engine misfires
problems.typicalMileage: 100,000+ miles — "exceedingly rare" on well-maintained B57 engines, but can occur in high-mileage or poorly serviced examples
problems.repairCost: £1,500–£3,500 (chain kit, tensioners and guides) — a complete timing chain replacement can cost £8,000–£12,000

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Unlike the infamous N47 diesel, the B57's timing chain is "notably robust and reliable" and issues such as timing chain stretch and guide failures are "exceedingly rare". However, in high-mileage or poorly serviced engines, timing chain stretch can still occur. BMW claims the timing chain is a "lifetime part" and does not service it, but regular oil changes and early attention to warning signs like rattling noises can help prevent costly engine damage. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/b57-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: DPF Clogging & Regeneration Issues
problems.symptoms: DPF warning light, loss of power, black smoke on acceleration, increased fuel consumption, "Drivetrain" warning message
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — short urban journeys accelerate the issue; a 2021 X7 diesel with only 11,339 km experienced DPF failure
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)
problems.recommendation: A 2021 BMW X7 diesel experienced power loss and emitted black smoke, with a DPF warning light indicating a failure to regenerate. The suspected cause was a faulty exhaust back pressure sensor, which can prevent proper DPF regeneration. Replacing the sensor and performing a forced DPF regeneration resolved the issue. If you primarily drive short distances, factor in regular motorway runs to allow passive DPF regenerations. If the warning appears, have the sensor and DPF system inspected.

failureLink.label: Read the full DPF clogging guide

problems.title: Oil Leaks (Valve Cover & Oil Filter Housing Gaskets)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell from engine bay, visible oil residue around the valve cover or oil filter housing, oil spots under the car, low oil level warning
problems.repairCost: £400–£1,200 (gasket replacement — valve cover or oil filter housing)
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection if you notice oil spots or burning smell

problems.recommendation: Valve cover gaskets and oil filter housing gaskets are known leak points on both petrol and diesel X7 models. Because the X7 engine sits deep within the bay, small leaks can be hard to spot until smells or smoke appear. If you notice a burning smell or oil spots, have the gaskets inspected promptly. Catching leaks early prevents oil contamination of other components and more serious engine damage.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide → /failures/b57-oil-leaks/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: X7 xDrive30d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Free under recall; straightforward replacement out of warranty
Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Piston-valve contact means full replacement required
Sensor replacement + forced regeneration — straightforward fix
Catch early to prevent oil contamination and engine damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This X7 xDrive30d? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with BMW-approved oil changes (BMW Longlife-04 spec)
* Confirmed EGR recall completion — this affects BMW diesel engines globally
* Evidence of regular motorway use (DPF health)
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — these are complex luxury vehicles
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle — this is a warning sign of timing chain tensioner wear (rare but documented)
* No proof of EGR recall work — this is a free fix; lack of it suggests neglect
* Poor service history — the B57 is reliable but needs proper maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The X7 xDrive30d's B57 engine is one of BMW's finest modern diesels — a 3.0-litre single-turbo inline-six with 265 hp and 620 Nm that delivers effortless performance for a full-size luxury SUV. The B57 is "celebrated for its robust performance, impressive efficiency, and excellent reliability". Unlike the N47 and early N57 engines, timing chain issues are "exceedingly rare". However, EGR cooler recalls, DPF sensitivity, carbon buildup, and oil leaks are well-documented issues. Buy with full service history, confirmed recall work, and evidence of regular long-distance driving — and you'll have one of the most refined and capable luxury SUVs on the road.

cta.label: → Compare X7 xDrive30d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW X7 xDrive30d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or slapping noise from the engine (timing chain warning)
* EGR Recall — Verify EGR cooler recall completion — this is critical and affects BMW diesel engines globally
* DPF Health — Check for DPF warning lights; short journeys are a red flag
* Service History — Including the B57 engine and confirmed BMW-approved oil changes
* Coolant Level — Check for frequent top-ups; inspect for EGR cooler leaks
* Oil Leaks — Look for oil residue around the valve cover and oil filter housing
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for EGR, DPF, or timing-related codes
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
